claim_boundary: This is a fresh, preregistered confirmation on one public tabular geographic shift. The finite-sample statement is per registered reference/bridge event, not a population-wide privacy or fairness guarantee; it does not cover post-certification drift or unregistered outputs.

data_protocol.external_split: Seeded two-thirds bridge and one-third untouched diagnostic fold within each source-label stratum.
data_protocol.frontier: Identity plus 12 official INLP, LEACE, R-LACE, TaCo, and MANCE++ edits; no proxy rows.
data_protocol.preprocessing: Train-only standardization and PCA to 128 dimensions or the input dimension, whichever is smaller.

ACSIncome-CA-TX.audit_source: binary sex; excluded from the released feature matrix

pass_conditions.complete_execution: All 5 seeds run every one of 13 official candidates.
pass_conditions.complete_reporting: Report every seed, candidate failure, abstention, diagnostic result, and selected certificate regardless of outcome.
pass_conditions.external_diagnostic: Treat Texas diagnostic results as held-out checks; do not use them to select a candidate or to estimate theorem coverage.
pass_conditions.strict_replay: Every raw receipt has a strict-v2 receipt and a matching independent replay.

phase: fresh ACSIncome California-to-Texas strict bridge confirmation

protocol.l4_policy: reoptimize the minimum-error L2 candidate on the same covered table

selection.primary: At utility threshold 0.40, select the feasible L=2 candidate with lowest strict bridge-certified worst source-label error; lexical tie break; otherwise abstain.
selection.secondary: Reoptimize L=4 only for that selected L=2 candidate without using diagnostics.

stopping_rule: Run all five seeds and all candidates. No outcome-based threshold changes, data replacement, early stopping, or selective reporting.

strict_replay.execution: Write each raw frontier receipt, deterministically replay every bridge and release with the locked strict-v2 arithmetic, then independently replay the strict receipts before reporting selections.
